---
title: "Projects"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

# Projects

## Park & Polish: Developing a Mobile Car Care Venture Through Market Analysis and Marketing Strategy

Park & Polish was developed as part of a New Enterprise Development project focused on identifying a real customer pain point and transforming it into a scalable business concept. The idea centred on a mobile car care service delivered while customers’ vehicles were already parked in locations such as shopping centres, airports, and business parks.

### Project Overview

This project explored how convenience, sustainability, and digital service design could be combined into a customer-focused business model. Instead of requiring customers to make separate time for car washing, the concept repositioned the service around existing parking time, making it more efficient and appealing for busy users.

### My Contribution

My contribution to the project included supporting the development of the business concept, analysing customer pain points, and helping shape the strategic logic behind the service model. I worked on clarifying the value proposition and considering how customer convenience, service positioning, and repeat-use strategies could support long-term growth.

### Key Learning

This project strengthened my understanding of customer-focused business design, value proposition development, and marketing strategy. It also helped me understand how market needs can be translated into a business idea with practical growth potential.

---

## Business Analysis and Process Improvement Project for a Cookie Manufacturing Company

This project involved a comprehensive business analysis of a cookie manufacturing company, with the objective of improving both production and sales processes.

### Project Overview

The work focused on identifying operational inefficiencies, calculating production costs, analysing profit margins, and evaluating opportunities to reduce costs across the supply chain. The goal was to better understand how production and sales decisions affected profitability and operational efficiency.

### My Role

I took a lead role in calculating production costs, analysing profit margins, and identifying cost-saving opportunities across the supply chain. This involved reviewing how ingredients, labour, and operational processes contributed to total cost and affected business performance.

### Tools and Methods

The project used business analysis tools such as:
- Cost-benefit analysis
- Process mapping
- Profitability analysis
- Operational review across sourcing, production, and delivery stages

### Outcome

The final recommendations aimed to improve efficiency, reduce unnecessary costs, and enhance profitability. This experience strengthened my analytical thinking, financial interpretation, and ability to turn business findings into practical recommendations.
